seoTitle: How to Add Multiple Businesses in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to add and manage multiple businesses in QuickBooks Online so you can keep separate books for each entity you run.
intro: Running more than one business often means keeping the books separate, and QuickBooks Online supports that through its multi-company structure. This guide covers how to add another business and switch between entities.

steps.heading: Go to your account settings
steps.body: Open the settings menu where you manage your QuickBooks Online subscriptions and companies.

steps.heading: Choose to add a new company
steps.body: Select the option to create or add another business, which typically requires its own subscription.

steps.heading: Set up the new company's details
steps.body: Enter the basic information for the new business, such as its name, industry, and fiscal year.

steps.heading: Switch between businesses as needed
steps.body: Use the company switcher to move between your different QuickBooks Online accounts without logging out.

faq.question: Do I need a separate subscription for each business?
faq.answer: Yes, each business generally requires its own QuickBooks Online subscription since they're treated as separate companies.

faq.question: Can I use the same login for multiple businesses?
faq.answer: Yes, you can access multiple company files from one login and switch between them easily.

faq.question: Can data be shared between the two businesses?
faq.answer: No, each company file keeps its own separate set of books, so data doesn't automatically flow between them.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many businesses I can add?
faq.answer: There's no strict limit tied to your login, though each additional company does require its own subscription cost.
